$comment: sizeof of a char literal folds to 4 (char-const is int, C 6.4.4.4) and of a string literal to N+1 (char[N+1], C 6.4.5): 4 + 5 + 33 = 42. The folds happen at the semantic/MIR tier (unevaluated operand); the exit reflects the folded sizes, so a wrong fold gives a loudly different exit. Also the runtime witness that (int)sizeof('c') no longer crashes the compiler (the lowerCast InvalidType-operand guard). Red-on-disable: revert the char/string literal typing -> H0009 sizeof-unresolved -> fails to compile.
